headline: Trump Financial Disclosure Shows 21,000 Trades in 2025
summary: (Bloomberg) -- President Donald Trump made more than 21,000 securities trades in his first year back in office, often in intense bursts tied to market events he created.Most Read from BloombergExxon to Change Name for First Time in Decades After RedomicileMeta Is Planning a Cloud Business to Sell AI Computing PowerGermany Rejects Trump’s Demands for NATO Loyalty to WashingtonRussia Indicates Ukraine Fired Long-Range Ballistic MissileKrafton Agrees to Pay ‘Subnautica 2’ Bonuses as Developer’s CEO

headline: General Dynamics Vs. Lockheed Martin: Buy General Dynamics for Deep Marine Backlogs
summary: General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) and Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) reported Q1 2026 results pulling the two defense giants in opposite directions. General Dynamics beat on submarines and Gulfstream jets. Lockheed leaned on a record backlog to explain a messy quarter marked by fresh program charges and a cash flow reversal. Submarines Carry GD. headline: Why Lockheed Martin (LMT) Is Up 6.1% After Massive Missile-Defense Contracts And Index Shift
summary: In late June 2026, Lockheed Martin was awarded a seven-year undefinitized contract action for up to US$35.00 billion to rapidly expand THAAD interceptor production, while also securing a separate US$3.00 billion U.S. Army contract for Sentinel A4 radar systems and being reclassified in several Russell equity indices. Together with recent expansions of its U.S. munitions manufacturing footprint and a positive analyst upgrade, these awards underscore how sustained demand for missile defense...
